title: Value Propositions for Sales
shortSummary: Stop selling features. Learn to articulate value in terms your buyer actually cares about — outcomes, ROI, and business impact.
whyItMatters: Buyers don't care about your product. They care about their problems. The gap between what your product does and what your buyer needs is bridged by your value proposition. Get this wrong and every conversation stalls at 'sounds interesting, let me think about it.' Get it right and buyers lean forward.
coreConcepts: Connecting product capabilities to buyer pain
Quantifying value: ROI, time saved, risk reduced
Tailoring your value prop to different stakeholders
The 'so what?' test for every claim you make
Building a business case that gets internal buy-in
